Name: curvature_aware_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing for Efficient Machine Learning Inference
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that incorporating curvature-aware precision routing into machine learning models will improve inference efficiency while maintaining accuracy, especially in resource-constrained environments.
Related Work: Recent works like Spectral Latent Steering have explored test-time optimization, but they often rely on heuristic control mechanisms and lack formal guarantees on numerical stability and computational overhead. Our proposal integrates spectral quantization theory with a curvature-aware precision routing framework, ensuring mathematical transparency and strict computational overhead bounds.
Abstract: This proposal introduces a novel curvature-aware precision routing framework for efficient machine learning inference. By combining spectral quantization theory with a mechanistic optimization proxy, we ensure mathematical transparency, explicit numerical stability analysis, and strict computational overhead bounds. Our approach prioritizes deterministic spectral estimators and coarse-grained precision tiering to guarantee reproducibility and scalability.
Experiments: Evaluate the proposed framework on benchmark datasets (e.g., ImageNet, CIFAR-10) using various machine learning models (e.g., ResNet, MobileNet)
Compare the performance of our approach with existing methods (e.g., Spectral Latent Steering, precision scaling) in terms of inference efficiency, accuracy, and computational overhead
Perform ablation studies to isolate the contributions of different components of our framework (e.g., curvature-aware routing, spectral quantization) and analyze the impact on model performance
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential complexity of integrating spectral quantization theory with curvature-aware precision routing
Need for careful calibration of precision tiering
Potential increased computational overhead due to added diagnostic loops
